heading: The Land That Was Left Behind
body: In August and September 2005, Israeli soldiers and police forcibly evacuated 8,600 Jewish settlers from 21 settlements in Gaza and four in the northern West Bank. The settlers — many of whom had farmed the coastal land for decades — resisted but ultimately left. Israel demolished the homes but preserved the synagogues. For the evicted settlers and their ideological heirs, the wound never closed.
caption: Former Gush Katif settlement zones in southern Gaza, 2005

heading: Trauma Becomes Ideology
body: The Hamas attacks on October 7 transformed the settler movement's political calculus. What had been a memory became a mission. Within weeks, veteran settlement advocates, religious nationalists, and politicians began publicly calling for the reestablishment of Jewish communities in Gaza — framing it as both a security necessity and a divine imperative. The attacks, they argued, proved that withdrawal had been catastrophic.
caption: October 7, 2023 — the day that reshaped the debate

heading: From the Margins to the Cabinet
body: Ministers in the governing coalition with roots in the settler movement held pivotal roles in the Israeli government. Their support for resettlement gave the movement unprecedented governmental access and financial leverage, despite opposition from military commanders and international partners. The January 2024 Jerusalem conference became a watershed moment — a movement that once held rallies at border fences was now holding court in ballrooms.
caption: Political spectrum of support within the Israeli coalition

heading: A Legal and Diplomatic Wall
body: International humanitarian law is unambiguous: an occupying power cannot transfer its civilian population into occupied territory. The UN Security Council, the International Court of Justice, and virtually every Western government have stated that Israeli settlement in Gaza would violate international law. Yet the legal barriers have done little to dampen the movement's ambitions — advocates contest the legal framing and argue the territory's status is uniquely contested.
caption: The International Court of Justice — guardian of the legal framework
